## Summary
Modified gravity theories (f(R), scalar-tensor, massive gravity, DGP braneworld) offer an alternative explanation for cosmic acceleration without invoking a dark energy component. These theories modify Einstein's general relativity on cosmological scales while recovering GR in the solar system through screening mechanisms.

## Evidence
The most studied models include Hu-Sawicki f(R) gravity, chameleon/symmetron scalar-tensor theories, and nDGP braneworld gravity. Current constraints from growth rate measurements (fσ₈) and gravitational slip (E_G statistic) are consistent with GR at the 10% level. Galaxy cluster abundance and weak lensing provide complementary tests through the modified relationship between lensing and dynamical masses.

## Significance
Modified gravity represents the main theoretical alternative to dark energy. Stage-IV surveys will test GR at the 1% level through combined probes. If the Hubble tension or S₈ tension are resolved by modified gravity rather than dark energy, it would represent a fundamental paradigm shift in our understanding of gravity on cosmological scales.
